title: Centaurea mariana
intro: Centaurea mariana, also known as mountain centaury, is a perennial plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, Centaurea mariana has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and expectorant properties. The plant's bioactive constituents include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. Centaurea mariana is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory complaints.
taxonomy: Centaurea mariana is classified within the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. This family is further divided into several subfamilies, and Centaurea mariana falls under the subfamily Carduoideae. The genus Centaurea itself is a group of thistle-like plants native to the Mediterranean region. Centaurea mariana is a species within this genus and is commonly known as the purple cornflower or a related name. The plant is native to Eastern Europe and parts of Asia.
